title: chrono and ulid are dead production dependencies in jmap-chat
description:
jmap-chat/Cargo.toml lists chrono and ulid in [dependencies] (production), but neither crate is imported or used anywhere in crates/jmap-chat/src/. Verified by grepping all src/*.rs files: zero 'use chrono' or 'use ulid' occurrences. Both are declared at the workspace level and pulled into the crate's prod dependency graph.

Impact:
  - Both crates add compile time for every build of jmap-chat and any crate that depends on it
  - chrono v0.4 with serde feature pulls in a non-trivial amount of code
  - ulid v1 is smaller but still dead weight
  - Downstream crates that compile jmap-chat acquire transitive dependencies they did not ask for

Fix: Remove 'chrono.workspace = true' and 'ulid.workspace = true' from crates/jmap-chat/Cargo.toml [dependencies].
If chrono is needed for future UTCDate parsing (RFC 3339 validation), add it back when the feature is implemented.
If ulid is needed for future client-side ULID generation (message_create client_id), add it back then.
The workspace-level declarations can remain for future crates to use.
